subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and professional structure with a detailed table of contents and consistent section headings
Logical progression through introduction, methods, univariate and bivariate analyses, model checking, and conclusion
Concise statistical narrative with mostly relevant content
weaknesses: Occasional redundant textbook definitions interrupt the flow
Minor numbering errors in tables and mid-sentence cut-offs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands were assessed at band 3 (coherence and conciseness) with organization at band 4.  The work is coherent and well organized, but minor digressions and formatting lapses prevent the top band.  A band 3 award accurately reflects the strong logical development and structure with occasional lapses.

strengths: Appropriate use of statistical notation (\bar x, Ï, r, RÂ², Ï) with variables defined
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, box-plots, histograms, scatter, residual, QQ and ranked scatter plots)
Good logical structure guiding the reader from data description through inference
weaknesses: Minor HTML formatting issues and inconsistent LaTeX notation
Some low-resolution images lacking axis labels and units
Typographical clutter in a few equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit firmly in band 3: mathematical language is largely correct, representations are effective, and communication is clear.  Inconsistent formatting issues and occasional low-quality figures prevent a band 4.

strengths: Evidence of independent thinking in choosing to retain outliers and switch to Spearmanâs Ï
Personal voice in contextual commentary on immigration patterns
Initiative shown by proposing extension to a global dataset
weaknesses: No novel methodological adaptations beyond syllabus procedures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach (band 2), but creativity and initiative remain at a basic level (band 1).  Overall, a band 2 accurately captures the engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on skewness, outliers, and normality test sensitivity
Thoughtful analysis of regression diagnostics (RÂ², SEE, ShapiroâWilk) linked to hypotheses
Comprehensive evaluation section suggesting non-linear models and broader data collection
weaknesses: Reflection sometimes remains descriptive rather than deeply critical
Could better connect findings to broader socioeconomic implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While initial reflections were at band 2, the depth of methodological critique and clear future implications elevate the work to band 3.  The student engages in substantial critical reflection and evaluation throughout.

strengths: Relevant use of descriptive statistics, Pearson and Spearman correlations, and linear regression diagnostics
Mathematics is fully commensurate with SL level and well applied across all analyses
Formulas for mean, correlation, RÂ² and SEE are clearly presented and interpreted
weaknesses: Minor computational inconsistencies (e.g., negative immigrant counts, contradictory p-values)
Lack of reported significance tests for some statistics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical tools are used appropriately and effectively, with a level of application that is fully commensurate with SL.  Minor computational errors prevent band 5; band 4 best reflects a strong, mostly correct understanding.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations follow a logical path from data description to inference.  Arguments are usually clear, though occasional typographical clutter (e.g., misplaced HTML tags) disrupts readability.  Overall clarity is good but not exemplary.

Formulas are applied correctly and numerical results are plausible.  Some inconsistencies (negative immigrant counts in the outlier list, mis-placed thousands separators) reveal minor misunderstandings, but overall understanding is good and errors are not pervasive.

The student provides numerical tables, box-plots, histograms, scatter and residual plots, QQ-plot and a ranked scatter plot.  These are all relevant to the analysis and generally labelled.  A few images are low-resolution and axes are sometimes lacking units, so the work is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear sequence: introduction and hypothesis, detailed plan, separate univariate and bivariate analyses, model checking, conclusion and evaluation.  Transitions between these sections are smooth and references to earlier work are made explicitly (e.g., using the earlier outlier test to justify decisions in the regression section), so the reader can follow the argument without confusion.

Minor lapsesâsuch as occasional digressions into basic textbook definitions and some duplicated explanations of skewnessâinterrupt the flow, preventing the piece from reaching âexcellentâ coherence.  Nevertheless, the logical progression is strong enough to meet the descriptor for a well-developed and coherent report.

The task applies textbook statistical techniques without extending or adapting them in a novel way.  The selection of tests is appropriate but conventional, so creativity and initiative are present only at a basic level.

The student interprets \(r\), \(R^2\), SEE and the ShapiroâWilk \(p\)-value, linking them to the hypothesis.  Analysis is thoughtful but not probing; for instance, conflicting normality evidence is noted but alternative models are not explored.

A separate evaluation section identifies sample-size limitations, omitted variables and linearity assumptions, and suggests broader data or non-linear models for future work.  This is a clear evaluation, yet it remains at the level of general recommendations rather than a comprehensive, future-oriented plan.

Reflection appears throughout: the student comments on skewness, the impact of outliers, and assesses model assumptions.  These reflections are meaningful but largely descriptive; they stop short of deep, critical reconsideration of methodology.

Statistical notation (\(\bar x,\; \sigma,\; r,\; R^2,\; \rho\)) is largely correct and variables are defined.  Minor formatting issuesâHTML escape sequences for superscripts, commas used as decimal points in raw data, and occasional inconsistent use of bold within equationsâprevent full consistency, but appropriate language is employed in most of the document.

A personal voice is evident in contextual commentary (e.g., geographical reasons for immigration patterns) and in the evaluation section outlining what the student would change.  This constitutes a clear personal approach but lacks the innovation needed for the top band.

Most paragraphs are purposeful, focusing on statistical reasoning or contextual interpretation, and irrelevant digressions are limited.  However, the report spans 25 pages and repeats textbook definitions (e.g., of median, IQR, residuals) that could have been referenced rather than reproduced.  These repetitions keep it from the highest level of conciseness but it remains predominantly relevant.

All mathematical tools (descriptive statistics, Pearson and Spearman correlations, simple linear regression, residual diagnostics) are directly relevant to the stated objective and are used appropriately.  No superfluous techniques are included.

The student chooses an original context, justifies personal interest, and makes independent decisions such as retaining outliers and switching to Spearmanâs \(\rho\).  These elements show significant independent thinking, though they remain within standard procedures and do not demonstrate outstanding novelty.

A full table of contents with page references, numbered/labelled tables and graphs, and consistent section headings give the work a professional structure.  Each major part (introduction, methods, analysis, reflection) is clearly demarcated, and the appendix is separated from the main narrative.

Within sections, sub-headings (e.g., âTest for outliersâ, âNormalityâ) guide the reader and make it easy to locate evidence for each claim.  The piece therefore meets the âexceptionally well-organisedâ standard.

The mathematics sits comfortably within the SL syllabus (statistical inference and correlation/regression).  It is neither below nor significantly beyond course level; therefore âcommensurate with course levelâ is satisfied rather than âfully commensurateâ or âcomprehensiveâ.

annotations.comment: The title succinctly defines the scope and context, giving a clear overview of the investigationâs focus.
The alternative hypothesis is stated too generically. Specify the expected positive direction (e.g., âa positive correlationâ) to clarify the testable claim.
The âUnivariate analysisâ heading is clear, but the accompanying description repeats earlier definitions. Streamline to avoid redundancy.
The formulas for the mean are correctly presented and clearly defined, demonstrating accurate use of appropriate notation.
Footnote citations use inconsistent formats and lack uniform style. Apply a consistent bibliographic convention to improve presentation.
The detailed table of contents is professionally structured with clear section headings and page references, enhancing navigation.
The description of the investigation plan ends mid-sentence. Review for incomplete thoughts and ensure each step description is fully articulated.
The data description section contains extensive definitions that disrupt flow. Consider summarizing or citing definitions to improve conciseness.
The raw data table includes ranking that may not directly support analysis. Consider ordering by variable value to focus readerâs attention.
The box-plot images are informative but axes labels and units are hard to read. Improve resolution and ensure axes are clearly annotated.
The list of outliers shows negative immigrant counts, indicating a sign error in calculation. Review boundary application for accuracy.
The contextual discussion of why certain countries are outliers reflects thoughtful personal engagement with the topic.
The Pearson formula includes extra parentheses that add clutter. Simplify to $$r_{xy}=\frac{\sum(x_i-\bar x)(y_i-\bar y)}{\sqrt{\sum(x_i-\bar x)^2\sum(y_i-\bar y)^2}}$$.
The student effectively uses correct statistical terminology (median, quartiles, IQR) to interpret distributional features.
The outlier boundary formula is presented as plain text. Enclose in $$...$$ to ensure consistent LaTeX formatting for clarity.
Include a quantitative skewness measure (e.g., skewness coefficient) alongside the qualitative description to strengthen the analysis.
Table 2 is labeled as âTable 5â in the caption, creating confusion. Ensure consistent numbering across tables.
The use of the population standard deviation formula is correct but the rationale for using n instead of nâ1 should be justified.
The residuals table contains many decimal places which clutter the presentation. Round to two decimal places to enhance readability.
The residual and Q-Q plots effectively illustrate homoscedasticity and near-normality, showing clear understanding of regression diagnostics.
The ShapiroâWilk table reports W=0.9157, p<.005 but text cites p=0.03114. Clarify which values are correct to avoid contradictory interpretations.
The discussion of test sensitivity and outlier influence demonstrates deep reflection on analytical versus visual normality assessments.
Presenting $$r=0.3721$$ without reporting a p-value limits interpretability. Include the significance test to contextualize the correlation strength.
The regression equation uses y instead of Å·, reducing clarity. Consistent notation (e.g., $$\hat y=0.93x+33.32$$) is recommended.
The RÂ² and SEE are clearly displayed and interpreted, showing that only 13.9% of variance is explained, which is wellâcommunicated.
For Spearmanâs formula, explicitly show the computation of \(\sum d_i^2\) and intermediate ranks to strengthen transparency.
The conclusion restates findings well but could better connect results to broader socioeconomic implications for stronger critical reflection.
The evaluation outlines clear limitations and suggests logarithmic or power regressions, demonstrating substantial critical reflection on methodology.
Proposing a global dataset expansion shows personal initiative and innovative thinking beyond the syllabus requirement.
